title: Antirrhinum mohavea
intro: Antirrhinum mohavea, also known as Desert Snapdragon, is a flowering plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. The health benefits of Antirrhinum mohavea include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Antirrhinum mohavea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Antirrhinum mohavea is classified within the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as the plantain family. This classification is based on its genetic and morphological characteristics. As a member of the Plantaginaceae family, Antirrhinum mohavea is closely related to other plant species such as Plantago and Veronica. The genus Antirrhinum itself is often included in the family Plantaginaceae, although some sources may group it in the Scrophulariaceae or Orobanchaceae families. Further research may be necessary to fully understand the classification of Antirrhinum mohavea.

The plant contains compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Its leaves and flowers are also used to treat coughs and respiratory issues due to their expectorant properties.
